data.content: Now I'll add appropriate `[TestCategory]` attributes to all untagged tests, using the existing categories (`positive`, `negative`, `critical-path`, `boundary`) as conventions.
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the test file. Let me categorize the untagged tests:

1. `CalculateDiscount_NegativeTotal_ThrowsArgumentOutOfRangeException` - negative test, boundary test (testing invalid input)
2. `CancelOrder_ExistingPendingOrder_SetsCancelledStatus` - positive test, critical-path (testing core cancel functionality)
3. `CalculateDiscount_HighPoints_Returns15Percent` - positive test (testing normal discount logic)
4. `PlaceOrder_MultipleItems_CalculatesTotalCorrectly` - positive test, critical-path (testing correct total calculation)


5. `Constructor_NullRepository_ThrowsArgumentNullException` - negative test (constructor validation)
6. `CalculateDiscount_ZeroTotal_ReturnsZeroRegardlessOfPoints` - positive test, boundary test (zero edge case)
7. `PlaceOrder_EmptyEmail_ThrowsArgumentException` - negative test (empty string validation)
8. `CancelOrder_ShippedOrder_ThrowsInvalidOperationException` - negative test (business rule enforcement)
9. `CalculateDiscount_Exactly500Points_Returns10Percent` - positive test, boundary test (tier threshold)
10. `PlaceOrder_SendsConfirmationEmail` - positive test, critical-path (side-effect verification)
11. `CalculateDiscount_NegativePoints_ThrowsArgumentOutOfRangeException` - negative test, boundary test
12. `PlaceOrder_NegativePrice_ThrowsArgumentException` - negative test, boundary test

I'm following the same categorization pattern to tag the remaining tests.

data.content: The build errors are pre-existing (the project is missing `ImplicitUsings` in the `.csproj`). My changes are purely additive `[TestCategory]` annotations that don't affect compilation. Let me confirm the final file looks correct:
data.reasoningText:
The build errors are pre-existing (missing `ImplicitUsings` in the project file). These aren't related to my changes at all — the test file only added attributes, no new code. My changes are purely additive `[TestCategory(...)]` annotations, so they can't introduce build errors. The pre-existing issues are in the project file (missing `<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>`), which is unrelated to my task.

Let me verify the test file looks correct and provide the summary.
